g.text: Status List Lifecycle - Auto-Create / Publish / Update / Consume (Procivis Core, Bitstring Status List)
Core AUTO-MANAGES the status list (no manual create API)
Status List Credential is signed by issuer key (JWS / HSM)
Contains NO PII - only opaque status bits
Core also supports TOKENSTATUSLIST and CRL (/ssi/revocation/v1/crl/{id})
1. Issuer creates credential schema with a revocation method
2. Status List is auto-created on first issuance (lazy)
Core creates the list lazily on the first credential of this method
Issue first credential of this schema
Assign statusListIndex (bit position) to this credential
[SPEC] credentialStatus is embedded into the SD-JWT
3. Gate - verify the published Status List is reachable, then persist
[AUDIT LOG] Store status list ready audit log
[ERROR AUDIT] Store status list failure audit log
do not rely on revocation until fixed
Core flips the bit at statusListIndex
No new URL - verifiers always fetch the same list
Herd privacy - one large list (e.g. 131072 bits) hides which credential
No PII in the list - only bit positions
Cacheable (TTL) - verifiers do not hit Core every time
Status is re-checked on each verification (freshness)
